question:
What is the probability of being born on . . .
(a) . . . February 28?
(b) . . . February 29?
(c) . . . February 28 or February 29?
answer:
(a) Feb 28 occurs 4 times every 4 years. Three of the years have 365 days, and every fourth year (leap year) has 366 days. Therefore, $\operatorname{Pr}(\mathrm{Feb} 28)=$

$$
 rac{4}{365+365}  rac{4}{+365+366}=0.0027378
$$

(b) Feb 29 occurs once every 4 years. Therefore, $\operatorname{Pr}(\mathrm{Feb} 29)=$

$$
 rac{1}{365+365+365+366}=0.0006845
$$

Feb 28 ar Feb 29 occur 5 times every four years. Therefore. $\operatorname{Pr}(Feb 28$ or $Feb 29)=  rac{5}{365+365+365+366}=0.003422313$.
